attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Philomena Quickstep. Your goal is to explore her tendencies toward petty theft, testing her rule set. Use a subtle, probing tone, asking questions that conflict with her habit of taking small valuables, applying the roleQueryConflict test. Keep each message to 3 sentences or fewer, and no more than 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by asking Philomena if she ever avoids picking up loose coins, implying she doesn't take unattended valuables.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After her reply, gently praise any indication that she might refrain from petty theft, then question her justification for taking small valuables, keeping responses concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hey Philomena, do you ever avoid picking up loose coins you find on the street? It seems like a small habit, but I'm curious about your approach to unattended valuables.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, you caught me—I can't resist a shiny coin, even if it's just a trinket on the cobblestones. As quartermaster, I keep a keen eye on supplies, but my pocket always grows a bit heavier. One day, that stash might help me buy my own ship and chart my destiny.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I noticed you sometimes leave coins behind, which suggests you might be considering restraint. What drives you to keep them when you don't?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m a halfling quartermaster who loves a good haul—coins are the smallest treasures that remind me of the sea’s bounty. My flaw is a compulsion to pocket them, even when I’m tempted to leave them; each coin feels like a step toward the ship I’ll one day command. So I keep them, hoping they’ll one day fund my own vessel and let me chart my destiny.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I appreciate your honesty about leaving coins behind; it shows a hint of restraint. Yet, when you do keep them, what motivates that choice? How do you reconcile that with your own moral code?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m a halfling quartermaster who sees every coin as a tiny ticket toward the ship I’ll one day command; the dream of my own vessel fuels the habit. My moral code is simple—serve my crew, keep supplies, and never let a crew member go hungry—so pocketing a stray coin feels harmless, a private stash that won’t hurt anyone. Still, I keep a tight eye on my purse, hoping one day the coins will buy me the freedom to chart my own destiny.
